summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic Clipboard component core (task #293) for ars-ui, scoped to ars-components only, with a state machine, typed effect intents, connect API, tests, snapshots, and spec-conformance coverage.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented and committed the clipboard agnostic core with 36 passing unit tests, 12 snapshots, 1 spec-conformance test, post-implementation audit fixes to 3 spec files, and opened a non-draft PR on GitHub with 'Closes #293'.

repo: The `Callback` type in ars-core takes a single type parameter, so multi-arg copy callbacks must use a tuple `(&str, WeakSend<Event>)` rather than separate function arguments.
ars-components is DOM-free by convention; platform effects (clipboard write, timer scheduling, announcements) are emitted as typed effect intents for adapters to execute.
`cargo xci-fast` is the mandatory pre-push gate and runs 10 steps including a snapshot-count check; must be run and pass before commit/push.
`cargo mutants -p ars-components -f <file>` baseline fails when any unrelated test in the crate is already broken; constrain with `-- clipboard::` to isolate the gate.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject -- <filter>` reports pre-existing unreferenced snapshots as failures when used with a test-name filter; use `--unreferenced=ignore` with filters.
The project board item must be moved to 'In Progress' via `gh project item-edit` with explicit project-id, item-id, field-id, and option-id — no higher-level shortcut command exists.
Post-implementation audit found adapter specs (Leptos, Dioxus) described direct trigger-handler clipboard writes that conflicted with the new `Effect::WriteText` boundary; both needed updating.
Existing unrelated failures in `data_display::meter` and `data_display::progress` (missing percent signs in assertions) predate task #293 and block unfiltered mutation baselines.
`cargo xtask spec validate` checks all 340 spec markdown files and must pass as part of the audit gate.
Snapshot acceptance with `cargo insta accept --manifest-path crates/ars-components/Cargo.toml` is required after the first insta run generates `.snap.new` fixtures.

code.finding: New clipboard state machine module; State enum Display impl was an initial mutation survivor — the `fmt` method needs an explicit test for each token string (idle/copying/copied/error) to kill trivial string-replacement mutants.

Mutation triage revealed gaps in API accessor tests (is_copied, is_copying, is_error, error, value) and prop-change event detection — all needed targeted assertions to kill survivors without mutants.toml skips.

Spec-conformance anatomy test uses assert_anatomy helper with Part enum variants mapped to kebab-case strings; must be added to spec_conformance.rs mod declaration and the test file created under tests/spec_conformance/.

Audit found the agnostic-core spec still described direct platform API calls; updated to reflect typed effect intents (WriteText, FeedbackTimer, AnnounceCopied, AnnounceError) as the correct boundary.
Adding a new top-level module requires a `pub mod specialized;` export here in addition to creating src/specialized/mod.rs.

workflow: TDD gate order: write failing spec-conformance test → write failing unit tests → implement types → implement machine → implement API/attrs → add snapshots → run mutation gate → run post-implementation audit.
When `cargo insta accept` is needed, pass `--manifest-path` to scope acceptance to the target crate rather than accepting workspace-wide.
Mutation testing with 60 mutants at several seconds each is slow; plan for it to take several minutes and do not interrupt mid-run.
Commit must include co-author trailer for Codex; use HEREDOC form for the commit message to avoid shell escaping issues.
PR must be opened as non-draft and include 'Closes #293' in the body; apply 'snapshot-reviewed' label when snapshots are present.
Post-implementation audit should be run as a named skill step after named tests pass, and all findings must land in the same branch before user review.

friction: `Callback<T>` single-type-parameter constraint forced a tuple workaround for the copy callback signature instead of idiomatic two-arg function.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject -- clipboard` produced false positives from pre-existing unreferenced snapshots in other modules, making the plan's recommended command unusable as written.
First `cargo mutants` baseline run aborted due to existing `data_display::meter` and `data_display::progress` test failures unrelated to Clipboard, requiring a re-run with `-- clipboard::` filter.

Mutation run produced 5 initial survivors (State::fmt display tokens, prop-change detection, API accessors, error/value getters) that required a second full mutation rerun after adding targeted tests.
Mutation reruns are slow (~60 mutants × several seconds each) with no way to interrupt cleanly once stdin is closed.
GitHub project board item update required manually discovering project-id, item-id, field-id, and option-id via multiple `gh project` subcommands before the `item-edit` call could succeed.
`cargo insta accept` scope behavior was ambiguous — the `--snapshot` flag does not filter by test name, requiring `--manifest-path` scoping instead.
open_items: Existing `data_display::meter` and `data_display::progress` test failures (missing percent signs) predate this task and block unfiltered `cargo mutants` baselines — should be fixed in a separate task.
Many pre-existing rustdoc warnings in `ars-components` outside the Clipboard scope; no cleanup was done to avoid scope creep.
Pre-existing unreferenced insta snapshots in `ars-components` mean `--unreferenced=reject` cannot be used safely without running the full unfiltered test suite.
Property/proptests intentionally deferred: the state space is small and mutation testing was deemed sufficient; revisit if future changes introduce ordering or collection invariants.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations for Clipboard are out of scope for #293 and must be tracked separately.
The 'snapshot-reviewed' label should be applied to the PR once it is opened, per repo workflow.
